title: lavandula stoechas subsp. lusitanica
intro: Lavandula stoechas subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Pyrenean lavender or Lusitanian dwarf lavender, is a subspecies of the Lavandula stoechas plant, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, pale purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils such as linalool and linalyl acetate. The health benefits of Lavandula stoechas subsp. lusitanica include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy. Lavandula stoechas subsp. lusitanica can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, which are commonly used to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
